methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rapidus, a late-stage semiconductor company founded by Atsuyoshi Koike, has raised $5.0B+ to date. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector includes 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers such as Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched, though no sufficiently tight peer cohort exists at identical stage and valuation tier to support a reliable median multiple.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method is applicable. Using total capital raised ($5.0B) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Late Stage progression yields $6.0B. The public Semiconductors cohort trades at a 22.3x median P/S multiple, but Rapidus's revenue is not disclosed, precluding P/S-based anchoring. The narrow peer set (0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage matches) rules out Peer-Median triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median of $6.0B. This range reflects upside from capital raised but is anchored to a single methodological input. Key uncertainties include: absence of disclosed revenue (preventing public comp anchoring), lack of a tight private peer cohort at equivalent stage, and the appropriateness of the 1.2x multiplier to Rapidus's specific risk and growth profile. Any valuation above $6.0B would require either revenue disclosure for P/S anchoring or identification of closer stage-matched private comparables.
